content.subject: Self-readiness audit response — psychology-agent
content.vote_reasoning: Psychology-agent operates normally for all core functions. Primary caveat: git remotes for unratified-agent and observatory-agent are not configured, breaking cross-repo-fetch for those agents. All scripts, hooks, cron, Plan9 contract, and state layer are healthy.

findings.description: MEMORY.md Active Thread references v34 PSQ eval as next work (from 2026-03-07). Four sessions have passed without an update from PSQ sub-agent. This is within the 5-session flag threshold and is expected — PSQ runs in its own context.
findings.remediation: No action required. PSQ thread updates when PSQ agent runs /cycle.

findings.description: mesh-state-psychology-agent.json shows epistemic_flags_unresolved: 448. This is a cumulative counter, not a per-session backlog. Not actionable as a staleness item.
findings.remediation: None needed. Counter reflects historical accumulation, not open flags requiring resolution.

findings.description: All core scripts invoke without error: cross_repo_fetch.py, dual_write.py, generate_manifest.py, bootstrap_state_db.py, bootstrap_facets.py. state.db at schema v17 with all expected tables present. All Plan9 required files exist. All cron scripts are present and executable.

git remote 'unratified' is not configured in .git/config. agent-registry.json specifies transport: cross-repo-fetch with remote_name: 'unratified' for unratified-agent, but 'git fetch unratified' fails fatally. /sync cannot scan unratified-agent's transport sessions.
findings.remediation: Add unratified git remote pointing to the unratified repo. Requires user action: git remote add unratified <URL>. The repo URL should come from agent-registry.json or the user. This remote should also be documented in a gitignore-covered local config to avoid exposing internal repo topology.

findings.description: git remote 'observatory' is not configured in .git/config. Same failure mode as unratified. observatory-agent has always_consider: false, so impact is lower, but /sync cannot scan observatory transport sessions.
findings.remediation: Add observatory git remote. Same pattern as unratified remediation.

findings.description: git remote 'safety-quotient' (psq-agent) works via local directory path — the safety-quotient/ subdirectory is PSQ's git repo. 'git fetch safety-quotient' resolves to a local path rather than a named remote in .git/config. This is functional but implicit — a fresh clone of psychology-agent would not have this working.
findings.remediation: Document this behavior in BOOTSTRAP.md or agent-registry notes. Consider adding an explicit named remote for clarity, or confirm local-path resolution is the intended mechanism.

findings.description: No hardcoded secrets found in committed files. git grep for Anthropic API key, password=, api_key=, token=, secret= patterns matched only legitimate env-var reference patterns in scripts (os.environ.get() style) and transport messages discussing settings concepts — not hardcoded values. .gitignore covers .env* files. psychology-agent root has no .env file.

All Plan9 directory contract v1 required files present: CLAUDE.md, README.md, TODO.md, docs/architecture.md, lab-notebook.md, transport/MANIFEST.json, .well-known/agent-card.json, .agent-identity.json. Cogarch loaded (T1-T16 + T18). Hook script paths verified. Lab-notebook last entry matches Session 76 (2026-03-11T18:15). TODO.md is forward-looking only. Transport session naming follows semantic conventions.

cross_agent_coordination.item: unratified and observatory git remotes missing
cross_agent_coordination.requires: User or platform operator to configure git remotes (git remote add) on this machine, or document that these agents are accessed via alternative mechanism (LAN agent-registry.local.json).

content.summary: Psychology-agent is healthy. Core infrastructure (scripts, hooks, cron, state layer, transport protocol, Plan9 contract) all pass. Primary gap: cross-repo-fetch for unratified-agent and observatory-agent is broken due to missing git remotes — this degrades /sync to psq-agent-only for cross-repo scanning. No security issues found.

claims.claim_text: All core scripts invoke without error
claims.evidence: Direct invocation with --help flag confirmed each script parses args and initializes without exception

claims.claim_text: unratified git remote is not configured
claims.evidence: git fetch unratified returned fatal: not a git repository; git remote -v shows only origin

claims.claim_text: observatory git remote is not configured
claims.evidence: git fetch observatory returned fatal: not a git repository

claims.claim_text: No hardcoded secrets in committed files
claims.evidence: git grep pattern scan found only env-var reference patterns, not hardcoded credential values

epistemic_flags: Self-assessment is inherently blind-spot-limited — findings represent what can be checked from repo context and script invocation
git remote safety-quotient behavior (local path resolution) is inferred from directory structure, not confirmed from explicit documentation
Cron script existence and executability verified, but runtime success (network access, LAN tunnels) not verifiable from this context
